feat: add HITL enforcement via verification gate

- Create hitl-verify.sh: programmatic check that HITL approval exists
  in feedback_history before allowing status transitions from locked
  states (designing_done, implementing_done, review_done, test_done,
  accepted)
- Update engine orchestration loop: mandatory 5-step HITL process
  (publish → collect → record → verify → advance)
- Add CRITICAL enforcement warnings to routing table
- Update init instructions template: HITL rules + codenook-engine ref
- Update init success message: mention codenook-engine for task mgmt

v0.2.32: Linear XP curve — 100 XP per level

commit.message:
Fix BearSSL: compile with musl GCC and fix link order

Two fixes for TLS compilation on Linux:

1. Release CI: compile libbearssl.a with the bundled musl GCC
   (not system glibc GCC) so the library matches the shipped compiler.
   Merged the separate Build/Inject steps into one step that runs
   after toolchain assembly.

2. Link order: move libbearssl.a after the source file on the
   command line. GCC's linker processes left-to-right — archives
   before the objects that reference them get their symbols discarded.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: pair API keys with regions in localStorage

Store API keys under region-suffixed localStorage keys (e.g.,
minimax-api-key-cn-beijing) so each region retains its own key.
Switching regions now auto-loads the saved key for that region.

Includes migration: existing keys saved under the old unsuffixed
key are automatically moved to the cn-beijing suffixed key.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add architecture and implementation plan

Defines the full stack architecture for WaCal — a zero-auth, link-shared
gig calendar with RSVP. Covers:

- FastAPI backend + PostgreSQL database schema
- React/Vite/Tailwind frontend (PWA, mobile-first)
- Docker Compose deployment model (following ppn pattern)
- Simplified CI/CD pipeline: build → manual approve → deploy to sufi
- API design and implementation task breakdown

commit.message:
feat: add RL Q-learning mode with mode selection in START.bat

New file: rl_drive.py
- Single-agent tabular Q-learning explorer (replaces broken rl_explore.py)
- Same connect/spawn flow as drive_forward.py:
    usePlayerVehicle first → spawn only if no vehicle present
- Uses existing server API: drive / state / reset / setTimeScale
- State: (grid_x, grid_y, heading_4) — 20m grid cells
- 5 discrete actions: straight, gentle left/right, hard left/right
- Rewards: +10 new cell | -0.1 revisit | -5 stuck | -15 fell
- Live ASCII map display in terminal (▲=agent  S=spawn  █=visited)
- Episode stats: reward, steps, cells explored, Q-states, epsilon
- Auto-save Q-table every 10 episodes (--save qtable.pkl)
- Resume training: --load qtable.pkl
- Args: --map --episodes --steps --timescale --load --save --no-launch

Updated: START.bat
- New step 2: Mode selection
    [1] WASD Steuerung  → drive_forward.py
    [2] Reinforced Learning → rl_drive.py
- RL mode prompts for episodes, steps/ep, time scale, optional Q-table
- Map selection moved to step 3
- Mod install step renumbered accordingly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add Public gallery flag to production extension manifest

The production extension was private on the Marketplace because
vss-extension.json was missing the galleryFlags property. Adding

commit.message:
feat: GitHub Pages deployment setup

- vite.config.ts: base set to './' for relative asset paths (works on any subpath)
- main.tsx: switched BrowserRouter to HashRouter (GitHub Pages has no server routing)
  - URLs become /#/, /#/project/:id, /#/docs
- .github/workflows/deploy.yml: GitHub Actions workflow
  - Triggers on every push to main + manual dispatch
  - Node 20, npm ci, npm run build
  - Deploys dist/ to GitHub Pages via actions/deploy-pages@v4

To publish:
  1. Create a GitHub repo and push: git remote add origin <url> && git push -u origin main
  2. Go to repo Settings > Pages > Source: GitHub Actions
  3. Wait ~1 min for the action to complete
  4. Visit https://<user>.github.io/<repo>/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Accept icon as base64 blob, data URI, or URL

normalizeIcon() converts raw base64 to data:image/png;base64,<data>
so Android clients can send the icon bitmap directly without hosting it.
data: URIs and http(s):// URLs continue to work unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: inject CURRENT_DATETIME into all spawn templates (#960)

* fix: inject CURRENT_DATE into all spawn templates (#960)

Agents spawned by the coordinator get a fresh LLM session with no
date context. The platform injects <current_datetime> into the
coordinator's session, but this was never forwarded to sub-agents.

Adds CURRENT_DATE: {current_datetime} to:
- Lightweight spawn template
- Full spawn template
- Scribe spawn template
- Explore agent inline prompt

Closes #960

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review — rename to CURRENT_DATETIME, sync all templates

Addresses review feedback from copilot-pull-request-reviewer:
1. Renamed CURRENT_DATE to CURRENT_DATETIME (value is full ISO timestamp)
2. Fixed explore inline trailing punctuation
3. Synced canonical .squad-templates/ and all 3 .template copies
4. Verified byte-for-byte parity across all 5 files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address Q review — coordinator instruction, scribe charter, usage guard

Q found 3 gaps in the CURRENT_DATETIME fix:

1. Added explicit 'pass current datetime' instruction to coordinator's
   'On every session start' block (line 107), parallel to team_root
2. Updated scribe-charter.md to use CURRENT_DATETIME instead of
   'today's date' / '{today}' for decision consolidation headings
3. Added DATES warning to full spawn template: agents must use
   CURRENT_DATETIME, never infer or guess dates

All 9 affected files synced via sync-templates.mjs.
Zero leftover CURRENT_DATE references (verified by grep).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use spawn.pickSpawnPoint for correct map default spawn position

Previous implementation used findClassObjects('SpawnSphere') which
never found anything because BeamNG spawn points are named scene
objects, not SpawnSphere class instances.

Now mirrors BeamNG's own spawn.pickSpawnPoint('player') fallback chain
(same order as levels.lua and spawn.lua):
  1. spawn.pickSpawnPoint('player')  — BeamNG's own function, preferred
  2. setSpawnpoint.loadDefaultSpawnpoint() → scenetree.findObject(name)
  3. PlayerSpawnPoints / PlayerDropPoints group → random member
  4. DefaultPlayerSpawnSphere (legacy compatibility)
  5. Current player vehicle position
  6. Hard-coded (0, 0, 130) last resort — should never be reached

Also: spawnCurrentVehicleType calls getDefaultSpawnPos internally
when no pos is supplied, so no hard-coded coordinates are needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: prefer current vehicle, use map default spawn on respawn

- New Lua command getDefaultSpawnPos: finds first SpawnSphere in the
  scene and returns its position + configurable Z offset (default +0.5).
  Falls back to current player vehicle pos, then hard-coded origin.

- spawnCurrentVehicleType now calls getDefaultSpawnPos internally when
  no explicit pos is provided -- no more hard-coded coordinates needed.

- drive_forward.py Step 4 reworked:
  1. Try usePlayerVehicle first -- if a vehicle is already in the scene,
     use it directly (no spawn, no interruption for the user).
  2. Only if that fails: query getDefaultSpawnPos from Lua, then call
     spawnCurrentVehicleType with that position.
  3. SPAWN_POSITIONS dict kept as last-resort static fallback only.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Landscape layout (lg: 1024px+): nav moves from bottom bar to left side rail,

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Start alt 2FA buttons hidden by default, reveal only if registered

Previously the TOTP/WebAuthn alternative auth buttons were visible by
default and only hidden after an async API call, causing a flash where
users could see and click them even when no 2FA was configured.

Now both the divider and button container have 'hidden' in the HTML
from the start. initAltAuthVisibility() removes 'hidden' only if
Authelia confirms the user has TOTP or WebAuthn registered.

This eliminates the confusing '2FA required' messages on the login
page for users with no 2FA configured.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: editable AI plans + manual recovery entry (#517)

**Closes #500: Make AI-generated workout plans editable**
- Added edit mode toggle in PlanDayDetailScreen TopAppBar
- Implemented EditableExerciseCard with inline editing for sets/reps/rest
- Added remove exercise functionality with delete button
- Added "Add Exercise" button to navigate to exercise library
- Save/Cancel controls persist changes to ActivePlanStore
- Plans marked as modified when edited
- Bilingual support (EN + ES)

**Closes #504: Manual recovery entry fallback**
- Manual recovery entry was already implemented in RecoveryScreen
- Added missing string resources for programs editing (reps_range, rest_sec)
- Manual entry shows when Health Connect is unavailable
- Sleep hours slider (0-12h) and readiness score slider (1-10)
- Recovery score calculated from manual inputs
- Bilingual support (EN + ES)

**Technical changes:**
- Updated PlanDayDetailContract with new intents: ToggleEditMode, RemoveExercise, UpdateExercise, SaveChanges, DiscardChanges, AddExercise
- Enhanced PlanDayDetailViewModel to manage edit state and exercise modifications
- Modified PlanDayContent to switch between view and edit modes
- Updated navigation to support exercise picker callback
- WorkoutPlan.markAsModified() preserves edit history

commit.message:
Add --verbose flag for debugging compilation issues

Shows step-by-step diagnostics: import resolution, lexing, parsing,
semantic analysis, codegen, temp dir creation, runtime source selection,
include dirs, BearSSL library discovery, and full C compiler command line.
Uses a global AtomicBool to avoid threading verbose through all compile
function signatures.

commit.message:
Hide alt 2FA buttons when no methods are registered

On page load, call /api/secondfactor/available to check which 2FA
methods the user has registered. If none (TOTP or WebAuthn), hide
the 'Alternative authentication' section entirely. If only one method
is registered, hide only the button for the missing one.

Previously these buttons were always shown, causing confusion when
visiting auth.lemoncode.dev directly — users saw TOTP/WebAuthn prompts
even though they have no 2FA configured.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: lazy-load Gemma-4, eager FunctionGemma, auto-download all required models (#202)

* feat: lazy-load Gemma-4, eager FunctionGemma router init

FunctionGemma (270M, CPU/XNNPACK) now initialises eagerly on chat open
so skill commands fire instantly without waiting for Gemma-4.

Gemma-4 (E4B/E2B) now lazy-loads only when sendMessage() routes to
PlainConversation or RouterNotReady — saves ~3-5s on chat open for
users who only need quick skill actions.

Adds isLoadingModel state + loading indicator in chat UI for when
Gemma-4 spins up mid-conversation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: mark FunctionGemma + EmbeddingGemma as required, auto-download in onboarding

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review issues in lazy loading and onboarding model tracking

- Skip gated models in ModelDownloadManager auto-queue when no HF token
- Track SP model download state in OnboardingUiState to gate allDownloaded
- Set isLoadingModel flag synchronously before coroutine dispatch in sendMessage()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: reset isLoadingModel flag after initGemma4(), not after full inference

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: allow HomePage to scroll by removing global overflow:hidden from body

- Remove body { overflow: hidden } — was blocking all scroll
- Split html/body/root rules: html+body keep height:100%, #root uses min-height:100%
- Editor Layout already enforces overflow:hidden via its own inline style (height:100vh)

feat: implement Iteration 0 (Design System & App Shell) and Iteration 1 (Sound Library & Simple Playback)

Iteration 0 — Design System & App Shell:
- Replace default Material 3 theme with Arcanum dark-only theme (gold/amber primary, black backgrounds)
- Implement Typography scale with serif display and sans-serif body styles
- Add motion tokens (ArcanumMotion) for standard animation durations
- Create ArcanumTopBar, ArcanumEmptyState, and PermissionGate components
- Implement MainBottomNavBar with 4 tabs (Home, Campaigns, Scenes, Library)
- Fix MainNavDestination: remove SETTINGS, add CREDITS_ROUTE
- Add Credits screen (Behind the Screen) with app identity, links, and tagline
- Update AndroidManifest label to Arcanum Audio
- Fix LocalTrackRepository to use assets/audio/ URIs

Iteration 1 — Sound Library & Simple Playback:
- Create SimpleAudioPlayer interface and ExoSimpleAudioPlayer implementation
- Add SimpleAudioPlayerFactory for testable DI via AudioPlayerModule
- Implement LibraryScreen with import FAB, file list, and play/pause preview
- Implement LibraryViewModel with in-memory file management and playback toggle
- Wire LibraryScreen into LIBRARY navigation route

Tests: 41 unit tests, 0 failures (added 30 new tests)
